commit.message:
docs: improve README with detailed architecture and third-party integrations

- Add comprehensive External Integrations section
- Add detailed system architecture diagram showing all layers
- Add integration overview diagram with data flow
- Document all 5 third-party services with:
  - Geyser (Yellowstone gRPC) - real-time streaming
  - Helius API - historical backfill
  - Metaplex JS SDK - NFT metadata
  - MongoDB - persistence
  - Elysia.js - HTTP server
- For each integration document:
  - Purpose and communication method
  - How it works with diagrams
  - API usage examples
  - Data flow explanation
- Add Transaction Lifecycle diagram
- Add Library Usage section with code examples
- Improve navigation with new table of contents
- Add emoji indicators for third-party services

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: refactor indexer as importable library

- Add public library API via src/lib/index.ts
- Export all services, types, and factory functions
- Configure package.json with exports field for library usage
- Add files field to control published files
- Add tsconfig.lib.json for library-specific build
- Create .npmignore to exclude source and dev files
- Refactor services to support both singleton and direct instantiation
- Make MetadataService constructor public
- Make ProcessorService accept options object
- Add BackfillConfig interface for configuration
- Update GeyserStreamingService with comprehensive JSDoc
- Add build:lib and build:all npm scripts
- Support dual-mode: standalone app and importable library

Library consumers can now:
import { GeyserStreamingService, MetadataService, ProcessorService } from 'solana-nft-indexer';
import type { Transaction, NFTMetadata } from 'solana-nft-indexer';

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

API, multi-tenant support, and Chainlink CRE signal recorder workflow.

commit.message:
Septons CRE signal recorder workflow

Cron-triggered CRE workflow that fetches AI signals from the Septons API,
reaches DON consensus on confidence/platform/direction, and writes
signed reports to SignalRegistry.sol on Ethereum Sepolia.

## Description
Applied exactly the changes specified in the "✅ FINAL AUDIT COMPLETE
(Feb 26 2026)" task:

1. Added missing root `.env.example`.
2. Appended AI-tool bloat ignores to the end of `.gitignore`.

using `git rm -r --cached`.

QUICKSTART`.
5. Added a `.nvmrc` pin to Node 22.

filters instead of npm run.

expected files have the correct contents.

  for clearer separation of lifecycle, detection, and config concerns

  dynamically only while inside a supported instance

Add Modular RAG analysis document (#159)

Summarize the evolution from Naive RAG to Modular RAG based on
a tweet by `@ihtesham2005` and the Gao et al. (2024) survey paper.
Cover three RAG paradigms, Self-RAG mechanism, key accuracy metrics,
and practical insights for production systems.

- 주요포인트: ~해요 → 개조식 (~임/~됨/명사구 종결)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Пати-данж: боты присоединяются к существующей forming пати

- find_forming_party() — ищет forming пати с местом
- Бот сначала проверяет есть ли forming пати, присоединяется к ней
  (даже если данж на КД у бота — чтобы все шли вместе)
- Только если нет forming пати — создаёт новую для данжа без КД

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Persist logged contacts in localStorage across page refreshes

The published CSV can take several minutes to update after a write.
localStorage now acts as an immediate cache: contacts are saved on
submit and restored on page load, filling the gap until the CSV
catches up. CSV data takes priority once loaded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enrich stocks panel layout: company names, daily context, range bars

Watchlist improvements:
- Show company name below ticker symbol to fill dead space and provide
  context. Names are cached in localStorage from search results, with
  hardcoded defaults for common tickers (AAPL, MSFT, etc).
- Change column now shows both percentage and dollar amount stacked,
  with "Day Change" column header so users know the time period.
- "Updated [time]" footer shows when data was last fetched.

Index cards improvements:
- Show both dollar change and percentage change (e.g. "+2.34 (+0.45%)")
- Daily range bar (low-to-high) with current price position indicator,
  colored green/red based on direction. Range labels show L/H values.
- Cards separated by subtle borders for visual rhythm.

Layout improvements:
- Vertical divider between indices and watchlist sections.
- Section headers ("US Markets" / "Watchlist") for clear context.
- Watchlist rows use two-line layout (symbol + company name) to better
  fill horizontal space.
- Responsive: stacks vertically below 520px, divider becomes horizontal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: fix local dev setup and purge bloat directories (#18859)

- **What:** Updated the local setup, removed bloat directories, and made
the dev experience more robust.
- **Why:** To make the local install deterministic and fast, ensuring a
clean repository environment without AI/IDE bloat directories.
- **Impact:** Faster clone times, smaller working tree, straightforward
setup with clear dependencies, and CI guardrails to prevent bloat
directories from returning.
- **Measurement:** Acceptance tests (smoke testing via `pnpm docker:dev`
and `curl` endpoints) demonstrated expected outcomes, with successful
startup of required database and application stack services.

Meta広告出稿準備: 神奈川県全域版v3画像6枚 + fbqイベント実装

- 広告画像v3: feed(1080x1080)+story(1080x1920)×3パターン=6枚（Pillow生成）
  - AD1: 「小田原・平塚」→「神奈川県」、97施設→44施設
  - AD3: 「神奈川西部」→「神奈川県」

- campaign_guide.md: ターゲット地域・画像パス・既存アカウント注意点・Pixel置換手順

- fbq ChatOpen: chat.js openChat()
- Pixel ID未設定時はtypeof fbqガードで安全にスキップ

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: timeout crash 처리 공통화 — calibration도 main loop와 동일하게 중단

기존:
  - _calibrate_seed(): RC_TIMEOUT 시 해당 시드 run만 break, 다음 시드로 계속
  - _timeout_crash 플래그가 calibration 중에는 절대 설정 안 됨 (dead code)
  → hung SSD에 계속 커맨드 전송하는 문제 발생

수정:
  - _handle_timeout_crash(seed, fuzz_data) 추가:
      stuck PC 분석 → dmesg 캡처 → crash 저장 → _timeout_crash = True
  - _calibrate_seed(): RC_TIMEOUT → _handle_timeout_crash() 호출 후 break
  - run() calibration 루프: _timeout_crash = True 되면 즉시 abort (기존 체크 활성화)
  - main loop RC_TIMEOUT: _handle_timeout_crash() 호출로 중복 코드 제거

commit.message:
Add automatic multi-session management via terminal-keyed file persistence

Sessions now "just work" — each terminal (tmux pane, iTerm tab, VS Code, etc.)
automatically gets its own browser session without any env vars or manual setup.
Session IDs are persisted to ~/.zenripple/sessions/ keyed by a hash of the
terminal's identifying env var (TMUX_PANE, ITERM_SESSION_ID, etc.).

- Extract shared session file helpers into mcp/zenripple_session_file.py
- Modify get_ws() priority: env var > in-memory > session file > create new
- Write-back session ID to file after successful connect (atomic temp+replace)
- Clean up session file + in-memory state on browser_session_close()
- Fix session replay to work with auto-session (_load_replay_state checks _session_id)
- Add ZENRIPPLE_CALLER_ID env var for sub-agent session isolation
- Add --write-file flag to zenripple_session.py
- Update SKILL.md: sessions automatic, sub-agent isolation docs, simplified examples
- Add 21 new tests (335 total), 4 rounds of code review clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Release/development to main (#62)

* Implement assistant setup and enhance indexed pipeline stages (#48)

* feat(init): add assistant-setup stage with API key and OAuth HTTPS support (#38)

Implement stage=assistant-setup in collab init that configures AI provider
authentication (codex, claude, gemini) with two methods: environment variable
references for API keys, and OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code + PKCE flow for
browser-based authorization. Zero new npm dependencies — uses Node.js built-in
modules (node:http, node:https, node:crypto) for the complete OAuth flow.

New files:
- src/stages/assistant-setup.ts — orchestration stage implementation
- src/lib/providers.ts — provider types, defaults, OAuth endpoints, helpers
- src/lib/oauth.ts — OAuth 2.0 PKCE flow with local callback server

Modified files:
- src/lib/prompt.ts — add promptMultiSelect, promptText, promptPassword
- src/lib/config.ts — extend CollabConfig with assistants field
- src/lib/orchestrator.ts — add options pass-through in StageContext
- src/commands/init.ts — wire stage, add --providers flag, update summary

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(oauth): address PR review — harden OAuth flow and token handling

- Add 15s timeout to token exchange HTTP request to prevent hanging
- Eliminate TOCTOU race by keeping callback server alive (single bind)
- Constrain token file paths to ProviderKey type to prevent traversal
- Explicitly chmod token files to 0o600 after write
- Handle corrupt token files gracefully (return null instead of crash)
- Auto-detect OAuth-only providers via client ID env vars
- Assert first run succeeds in resume test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(init): skip compose-mode prompt when mode is file-only (#42)

When file-only mode is selected, the compose generation mode prompt is
irrelevant since infra and MCP stages are skipped. Default to consolidated
silently instead of prompting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(init): generate MCP snippets only for enabled providers (#43)

- Rename stage codex-config → mcp-client-config
- Generate per-provider snippets (TOML for Codex, JSON for Claude/Gemini)
- Skip snippet generation if no providers are configured
- Remove setupCodexConfig prompt — auto-detect from enabled providers
- Rename --skip-codex-config → --skip-mcp-snippets

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(init): skip compose-generation and mcp-client-config in file-only mode (#44)

file-only mode has no Docker/MCP infrastructure, so these stages are
now skipped with informational log messages. Adds test coverage for the
new behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(init): add canon-scaffold and canon-ingest stages (#45)

- New `canon-scaffold` stage generates the canonical architecture scaffold
  in `docs/architecture/` with governance, knowledge, domains, contracts,
  and evolution directories. Runs in both file-only and indexed modes.
- New `canon-ingest` stage sends architecture files to MCP for Qdrant +
  Nebula ingestion. Only runs in indexed mode.
- Add `architectureDir` to `CollabConfig` (default: `docs/architecture`).
- Template files stored in `src/templates/canon/` as TypeScript exports.
- Scaffold does NOT overwrite existing files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(init): add AI-powered repository analysis stage (#46)

- New `repo-analysis` stage uses AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini)
  to analyze the repository and generate canonical architecture files
  (axioms, decisions, conventions, anti-patterns, domains).
- New `ai-client.ts` — minimal HTTPS AI client using only Node.js built-ins.
- New `repo-scanner.ts` — scans repository structure, detects stack/framework.
- System prompt stored as TypeScript template in `src/templates/canon/`.
- Add `--skip-analysis` flag to skip AI analysis.
- Only overwrites files containing `<!-- AI-GENERATED: PLACEHOLDER -->` marker.
- Runs in both file-only and indexed modes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(init): add CI workflow generation stage (#47)

- New `ci-setup` stage generates GitHub Actions workflows for architecture
  documentation maintenance.
- PR workflow (both modes): validates architecture on pull_request.
- Merge workflow (indexed only): re-ingests docs into Qdrant+Nebula on push.
- Templates stored in `src/templates/ci/`.
- Add `--skip-ci` flag to skip CI workflow generation.
- Does NOT overwrite existing workflow files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(oauth): validate OAuth client_id and warn about registration

Add validation to reject email addresses and API keys as OAuth client_id
values, with clear error messages explaining that a client_id is an
application identifier obtained by registering an OAuth app with the
provider. Also adds a warning that OAuth requires a registered
application and recommends api-key auth for standard API usage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(oauth): default to api-key auth, mark OAuth as advanced

Non-interactive mode now always uses api-key auth since OAuth requires
a registered application that most providers don't offer self-service.
Interactive mode labels OAuth as "advanced — requires registered OAuth
app" to set proper expectations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(init): implement file-only pipeline with dual-pipeline architecture (#38)

Refactor `collab init` into two explicit stage pipelines (file-only: 8 stages,
indexed: 12 stages) with no mode-branching inside individual stages.

Key changes:
- Add copilot as 4th AI provider (gh CLI-based, no API key)
- Add ANSI terminal output utilities (zero-dependency colors, stage numbering)
- Add canon-sync stage (full copy of collab-architecture to docs/architecture/uxmaltech/)
- Add repo-scaffold stage (project-specific architecture templates)
- Add repo-analysis-fileonly stage with docs/ai/ helper generation
- Add agent-skills-setup stage (Agent Skills Spec + Copilot instructions + CLAUDE.md)
- Extract shared repo-analysis helpers into repo-analysis-helpers.ts
- Replace OAuth module with streamlined credentials management
- Add config path expansion (uxmaltechDir, repoDir, aiDir)
- Fix canon sync race condition (git reset --hard vs pull --ff-only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: add guard-issues workflow to restrict issues to collaborators

Auto-closes and locks issues opened by non-collaborators while repos
are public but not yet ready for community contributions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(init): implement indexed pipeline with mode-aware agent configuration (#38)

Complete the indexed pipeline (14 stages) with infrastructure support:
- Phase A (shared): preflight → environment-setup → assistant-setup → canon-sync → repo-scaffold → repo-analysis → ci-setup → agent-skills-setup
- Phase B (infra): compose-generation → infra-start → mcp-start → mcp-client-config
- Phase C (ingestion): graph-seed → canon-ingest

Key changes:
- Extract generateAiHelpers to shared repo-analysis-helpers module
- Update indexed repo-analysis to write to repoDir and generate docs/ai/ helpers
- Make agent-skills-setup mode-aware: file-only agents read local files,
  indexed agents use MCP tools (context.scopes.list.v2, context.vector.search.v2,
  context.graph.degree.search.v2) plus local helpers
- Add graph-seed stage (seeds NebulaGraph via docker compose exec)
- Clean up canon-ingest (remove dead file-only check)
- Remove ingest-bootstrap stage and --ingest flag
- Replace canon-scaffold with canon-sync in indexed pipeline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(init): add workspace multi-repo support and phase clear-screen headers (#38)

Add --repos flag and auto-discovery for multi-repo workspaces. When cwd
contains ≥2 child git repos, init runs a three-phase pipeline:
workspace-level setup, per-repo analysis loop, and optional infra.
Each wizard phase now clears the screen with a descriptive title banner
for a cleaner UX flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(init): add `collab init infra` phase shortcut (#38)

Allow running only the infrastructure stages (compose-gen, infra-start,
mcp-start, mcp-config, graph-seed, canon-ingest) via `collab init infra`.
Requires an existing indexed-mode config from a prior full init run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(init): allow `collab init infra` on fresh hosts without prior config

Remove the requirement for an existing .collab/config.json. When no
config exists, bootstrap one with indexed mode automatically. When a
file-only config exists, override to indexed since infra always implies
indexed mode. This supports dedicated infrastructure servers that have
no repos.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(compose): bind MCP container to 0.0.0.0 for Docker port mapping

The MCP server defaulted to 127.0.0.1 inside the container, making it
unreachable from the host via Docker port mapping. Set MCP_HOST=0.0.0.0
in both compose templates so the health check endpoint is accessible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(infra): support shared infrastructure across multiple project repos

- Add health pre-checks to infra-start and mcp-start stages: probe
  health first and skip docker compose up when services already running
- Switch graph-seed and canon-ingest to use `docker exec collab-mcp`
  instead of `docker compose exec mcp` so commands work regardless of
  which compose project started the containers
- Make all Docker volumes and networks `external: true` in compose
  templates to allow sharing across project directories
- Pre-create Docker network and volumes in compose-generation stage
- Add --max_sessions_per_ip_per_user=10000 to graphd to prevent
  NebulaGraph session exhaustion from health probes
- Refactor buildIndexedPipeline() to reuse buildInfraStages() instead
  of duplicating infrastructure stages inline

Tested: collab init --mode indexed on api-server repo connecting to
infrastructure already running from a different project directory.
All 14 stages pass, all 124 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): resolve 59 ESLint errors and test race condition

Lint fixes:
- Add type assertions on JSON.parse calls (ai-client, cli-detection,
  model-listing, repo-scanner, repo-analysis, repo-analysis-fileonly)
- Replace require() with dynamic import() in providers.ts
- Replace require('node:child_process') with top-level import in
  assistant-setup.ts
- Remove unused imports (checkGhAuth, CliInfo, StageContext)
- Remove async from functions without await (update-canons,
  configureCopilotProvider)
- Add str() helper in repo-analysis-helpers.ts for safe unknown→string
  coercion in template literals
- Wrap catch(err) in String() for restrict-template-expressions

Test fix:
- Isolate COLLAB_HOME per test via mkdtempSync to prevent git lock
  conflicts when canon-sync runs in parallel CI tests
- Update autoDetectProviders test for async return type

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(e2e): add mock scripts for graph-seed and canon-ingest stages

The E2E test uses a mock MCP image when the real collab-architecture-mcp
repo is unavailable in CI. The mock was missing scripts/seed-graph.mjs
and npm run ingest:v2, causing the graph-seed and canon-ingest pipeline
stages to fail with "Cannot find module" errors.

Add stub scripts that exit successfully (no-op) so the full init pipeline
completes end-to-end even with the fallback mock image.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

The graph-seed and canon-ingest stages now use direct HTTP API calls
(POST /api/v1/seed/graph and POST /api/v1/ingest) instead of docker
exec. The mock MCP server used in E2E tests was missing these
endpoints, causing a 404 failure in CI.

The mock /api/v1/ingest endpoint was returning { ingested, skipped }

commit.message:
feat: Phase 44-46 Documentation - Documentation guide, Next.js 16 migration, operations guide

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

add persistent browser session and new CLI commands

Introduces a daemon model: a detached Node.js server owns a visible
Chromium window over localhost TCP. All browser commands auto-start the
session and route through the server.

New commands: go, snap, shot, log, eval, click, fill, wait, stop,
status, test, ls, clean. Positional psnap <url> replaces --url/--out
flags for stateless mode. Smart output routing (2 KB default threshold)
keeps agent context small. Network recording to JSONL with body preview
and JSON sidecar. Console circular buffer (500 entries). Playwright test
runner integration via psnap/fixtures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: security scrub — pin CI image tags, remove TLS bypass flags

Pin all CI template container images to specific versions to prevent
supply chain attacks via mutable :latest tags (13 images pinned).
Remove --skip-tls-verify from kaniko, --insecure from ArgoCD login
and trivy image scan — use --registry-certificate for private CA
trust instead. Remove :latest push destination from kaniko build.
Pin CNPG PostgreSQL image from :17 (major) to :17.6 (patch).

Add zelligent nuke command (#65)

* Add optional patched Zellij build to dev-install.sh

When ZELLIGENT_ZELLIJ_SRC points to a local Zellij checkout,
dev-install.sh now builds and installs it to ~/.local/bin/zellij.
Also uninstalls Homebrew Zellij to avoid PATH conflicts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add zelligent nuke command to fully delete sessions

Adds `zelligent nuke` to delete the current repo's Zellij session and
prevent resurrection. This goes beyond `zellij delete-session --force`:

- Force-kills lingering server/client processes (SIGKILL) that survive
  socket deletion and keep re-serializing session state
- Removes the resurrection cache directory so `list-sessions` won't
  rediscover the session
- Supports both macOS and Linux cache paths
- Idempotent: always succeeds even if no session exists

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Sandbox nuke tests to prevent touching real filesystem

Mock ps, kill, and sleep, and redirect HOME/XDG_CACHE_HOME/TMPDIR
to temp directories so nuke tests cannot kill real Zellij processes
or delete actual cache files.

commit.message:
T11: Infini-attention compressive memory baseline

Implements Control 3 (strongest non-reservoir memory baseline) from the
implementation plan: Infini-attention added to Qwen3.5-0.8B-Base.

Key components:
- src/models/infini_attention.py: Core implementation
  - CompressiveMemory: per-head associative matrix memory (M, z)
    with ELU+1 feature map, retrieve/update ops
  - InfiniAttentionLayer: wraps any attention module; adds memory
    retrieval side-path with Q/K/V adapter projections and learned
    beta mixing gate; zero-init so training starts from base model
  - apply_infini_attention(): patches model layers in-place
  - reset_infini_memory(), get_infini_trainable_params() helpers
  - InfiniAttentionConfig dataclass
- scripts/train_infini_attention.py: 5K-step training script
  (LoRA + infini-attention adapters, FineWeb data, wandb logging)
- scripts/eval_infini_attention.py: evaluation script writing
  results/baselines/infini_attention.json
- results/baselines/infini_attention.json: placeholder (populate
  after GPU training)
- tests/test_infini_attention.py: 32 unit tests (all passing)

Paper: Munkhdalai et al., 2024, arXiv:2404.07143

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Filter cross-streets from walk coverage using overlap length

ST_DWithin alone matches any road within 8m of the walk path, including
cross-streets at every intersection (they pass within 8m but the walker
never travelled along them). Added a second condition: at least 20m of
the way must fall inside the 8m walk buffer. A perpendicular cross-street
only overlaps the buffer for ~16m, so it gets filtered out. A road the
walker actually travelled along overlaps for the full segment length.

commit.message:
Clarify CDCOnBoot FQBN as example, not hardcoded value

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add temperature-scaling calibration (Platt-style) (#578)

* Remove chain features and increase model regularization

Chain features (chain_ball{n}) used actual drawn values during
training but predicted values at inference — an exposure-bias
mismatch that inflated test accuracy without improving real-world
performance. The multi-output stacking model already captures
cross-ball dependencies without this leakage.

Regularization tightened to reduce mode dominance:
- RF: max_depth 10→6, min_samples_leaf 5→20, min_samples_split 5→20
- HGBC: max_depth 6→4, min_samples_leaf 20→50

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add probability smoothing and increase regime temperatures

Two changes to reduce mode dominance (Ball4=33 in every run):

1. Uniform mixture smoothing in _sample_from_proba: blends model
   probabilities with a uniform prior before temperature scaling
   (Dirichlet smoothing). Default prediction_smoothing=0.3 means
   30% weight on uniform — reduces Ball4=33 from ~100% to ~45%
   probability per run. Configurable via prediction_smoothing key.
   Also fixes confidence reporting to reflect the actual post-scaling
   probability of the chosen class rather than raw model max.

2. Default regime temperatures raised from (0.6, 1.0, 1.4) to
   (0.8, 1.2, 1.6) for broader exploration without sacrificing
   low-entropy regime focus. Overridable via regime_temperatures key.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add temperature-scaling calibration (Platt-style) to predictor

Adds a per-ball post-hoc calibration step after model training:
- _calibrate_temperature(): grid-searches T in [1.0, 8.0] to minimise
  NLL on a chronological held-out calibration slice (15% of train data).
  T >= 1.0 enforced so calibration only softens overconfident models;
  sharpening (T < 1) is excluded to preserve smoothing diversity.
- Calibration split is chronologically after the fit data (no leakage).
  Feature pruning is performed on the fit portion only.
- Calibrated temperatures saved to <gamedir>/models/calibrated_temps.json
  and reused on load (no recalibration unless force_retrain).
- In generate_predictions: effective_temp = cal_temp * (regime_temp / 1.2)
  so regime scaling is applied multiplicatively around the calibrated base.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update README: fix coverage badge and document optional config fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update CLAUDE.md: model architecture, temperatures, calibration, optional fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(flow-frontend): guard all array accesses against undefined

- DispatchPanel: earningsEstimate ?? [0, 0] before index access
- Dashboard: (flowState.upcoming ?? []).map(...)
- Dashboard: (flowState.peaks ?? []).map(...)
- Dashboard: (flowState.signals ?? []).map(...)

API returns incomplete FlowState where arrays can be undefined.
All direct .map() and [index] accesses are now guarded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix book-appointment: check access_token for OAuth GHL connections

The guard `const accessToken = integration.api_key` always returned null
for OAuth-connected GHL integrations (which store the token in
access_token, not api_key). This caused "Calendar not properly
configured" on every booking attempt even though calendar-check worked
fine (it delegates to GoHighLevelIntegration which handles both fields).

commit.message:
fix: ME_r1 partner_weights feedback loop (#536)

* fix: ME_r1 partner_weights to prevent positive feedback loop

partner_bid_level: 1.0→0.5 (0-10 scale was doubling the score),
partner_passed: 1.0→-1.0 (should penalize, not reward),
remove partner_bid_confidence (redundant with partner_bid_level).

Gate X3 STOP traced to ME_r1 bidding 10 on 78% of hands due to
runaway partner signal amplification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update comparator configs with same ME_r1 partner_weights

Ensures all 5 R1 configs use identical modeloespecifico_r1 definition
(eval, H2H, dual comparator, legacy comparator).
